title: silene inflata subsp. thorei
intro: Silene inflata subsp. thorei, also known as Thore's catchfly, is a flowering plant species in the caryophyllaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, and has been used in herbal medicine to treat colds and coughs. Herbal preparations of Silene inflata subsp. thorei typically involve drying and infusing the plant in hot water to create teas or infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
